content.content:
1	# The Surveying Cascade — Patron-Vision Authoring through Mandate Dispatch
2	
3	Status: **Draft**
4	
5	> **⚠️ Cross-apparatus design.** This document defines the
6	> end-to-end cascade by which patron-authored product visions become
7	> commissionable mandate work, spanning three apparatuses: the
8	> Cartograph (data layer, shipped), the Surveyor (substrate +
9	> extension + default, not yet built), and the Reckoner (petition
10	> scheduler, shipped). It is a settled architectural reference; the
11	> open questions section names the design problems that remain.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Scope
16	
17	The cascade covers:
18	
19	- **Vision authoring on disk** — patrons author visions as `vision.md`
20	  files in a guild directory tree; `nsg vision apply` snapshots them
21	  into the cartograph.
22	- **Recursive surveying** — typed survey writs at every cartograph
23	  layer (vision/charge/piece) gate the work of decomposing one node
24	  into its children, using the Reckoner for scheduling.
25	- **Mandate emission** — surveying piece-layer (or charge-layer)
26	  rigs ultimately produce mandates, which dispatch through the
27	  existing implementer pipeline unchanged.
28	
29	Out of scope: the patron walkthrough surface (the patron-contract
30	validation step at the charge layer) is acknowledged as a
31	substantial design problem but designed elsewhere.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 1. Vocabulary
36	
37	| Concept | Definition |
38	|---|---|
39	| **Vision** | Long-lived patron intent. Authored on disk; snapshotted into a writ + VisionDoc by the cartograph. |
40	| **Charge** | First decomposition under a vision. Patron-contract boundary; the unit of patron walkthrough. Does NOT self-nest. |
41	| **Piece** | Recursive internal organization under a charge. Self-nests. No patron contract. |
42	| **Mandate** | Existing leaf writ; dispatched by Spider via the existing implementer pipeline. |
43	| **Survey writ** (`survey-vision`/`survey-charge`/`survey-piece`) | Typed writ that captures one act of surveying one cartograph node. `writ.body` holds the rig's notes (set on rig completion). One survey writ per surveying event. |
44	| **SurveyDoc** | Companion doc for a survey writ, keyed by writ id. Holds envelope metadata only — the rig's notes live in `writ.body`. |
45	| **Surveyor** | The role / agent / apparatus that performs surveying. The substrate provides the cascade machinery; concrete surveyor implementations register their rig templates as kit contributions. |
46	| **`ext['surveyor']`** | Plugin-keyed `writ.ext` slot owned by the surveyor-apparatus. Carries priority hints (severity, deadline, decay, complexity) on cartograph nodes — set by the patron via apply CLI for visions, set by the upstream surveyor rig for charges and pieces. The substrate translates these into Reckoner dimensions when emitting the survey petition. |
47	
48	> **Naming note.** "The Surveyor" was previously reserved in the
49	> framework architecture for a codex-awareness apparatus. That use is
50	> superseded by the cartograph-decomposition meaning defined here. The
51	> alias registry in the sanctum carries the substitution.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 2. End-to-end flow
56	
57	```
58	PATRON
59	  │ writes vision.md + sidecar in <GUILD>/vision/<slug>/
60	  │
61	  │ runs `nsg vision apply <slug> [--severity ...] [--deadline ...]`
62	  ▼
63	CARTOGRAPH (data layer)
64	  │ creates or updates the vision writ + VisionDoc (one transaction)
65	  │ writes ext['surveyor'] priority hints from CLI flags + sidecar
66	  │ emits Stacks CDC: book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}
67	  ▼
68	SURVEYOR-APPARATUS (substrate)
69	  │ CDC observer fires
70	  │ reads ext['surveyor'] hints from the cartograph node
71	  │ creates a survey-vision writ in `new` phase, parentId = the vision
72	  │ stamps ext['reckoner'] (priority dimensions derived from hints + defaults)
73	  ▼
74	RECKONER
75	  │ holds petition, evaluates per tick
76	  │ dependency-aware gating; scheduler decides accept/decline/defer
77	  │ on accept: clerk.transition → survey-vision writ moves to `open`
78	  ▼
79	SPIDER (extended to dispatch survey-* writ types)
80	  │ resolves rig template via the surveyor registry
81	  │ dispatches to the registered surveyor's rig
82	  ▼
83	SURVEYOR RIG (provided by the registered surveyor implementation)
84	  │ reads parent (vision) via writ.parentId
85	  │ reads existing children if any (re-survey case)
86	  │ reasons about decomposition
87	  │ creates child charges via cartograph API + sets ext['surveyor'] hints
88	  │ writes its notes into survey-vision writ.body
89	  │ completes the survey-vision writ
90	  ▼
91	[CASCADE]
92	  │ each new charge fires CDC → substrate creates survey-charge writ
93	  │ → recursion descends one layer
94	  ▼
95	LEAF (mandate)
96	  │ pieces eventually elaborate to mandates
97	  │ mandates dispatch through the existing implementer pipeline
98	```
99	
100	---
101	
102	## 3. Architecture
103	
104	### 3.1 Vision authoring on disk
105	
106	The patron's authoring surface is a directory tree:
107	
108	```
109	<GUILD>/vision/
110	  <slug>/
111	    vision.md             ← prose, patron-edited
112	    vision-metadata.yml   ← sidecar; carries `visionId` after first apply
113	```
114	
115	- The slug is a directory affordance, not a durable identity. After
116	  first apply, the sidecar's `visionId` field binds the file tree to
117	  its cartograph writ — written by the system, never edited by hand.
118	- `nsg vision apply <slug>` is a single code path for both first
119	  import and Nth re-import: locate sidecar, resolve bound writ via
120	  `visionId` (or create new), copy prose into `writ.body`, sync
121	  stage/codex via `transitionVision`, write priority hints into
122	  `ext['surveyor']`, and on first run write the new id back into the
123	  sidecar.
124	- Data flow is **one-way: file → writ**. The writ is a snapshot of
125	  patron intent; the file is the editable source.
126	- Stale-binding (file deleted, writ remains): warn at scan, error on
127	  next apply attempt; the patron transitions the writ to
128	  sunset/cancelled explicitly.
129	
130	### 3.2 Symmetric typed-writ-driven cascade
131	
132	Surveying happens through dedicated survey writs at every cartograph
133	layer. The cascade has a uniform shape:
134	
135	1. A cartograph node lands (vision via apply; charge or piece via an
136	   upstream survey rig).
137	2. CDC fires on the cartograph book — `created` or `updated`.
138	3. The surveyor-apparatus's observer reads `ext['surveyor']` from the
139	   node and creates the appropriate survey writ in `new` phase with
140	   `parentId` set to the cartograph node. Priority hints translate
141	   into Reckoner dimensions.
142	4. The observer stamps `ext['reckoner']` to enter the petition queue.
143	5. Reckoner gates per its existing rules.
144	6. On accept, Spider dispatches the registered surveyor's rig.
145	7. Rig reads the parent (and existing children, on re-survey),
146	   reasons about decomposition, creates children via the surveyor
147	   tool surface (which atomically creates the cartograph node and
148	   stamps its `ext['surveyor']`), writes its notes into the survey
149	   writ's `body`, completes.
150	8. Each child cartograph node fires fresh CDC → loop to step 2 one
151	   layer down.
152	
153	Why symmetric (separate survey writs at every layer) over asymmetric
154	(rigs bound directly to charge/piece writs): notes have a clean home
155	(`writ.body` of a dedicated writ per surveying event); charge/piece
156	writ lifecycle stays clean (their phase is about contract lifecycle,
157	not about whether decomposition has happened); future re-survey of an
158	existing charge is structurally trivial; substrate code is uniform
159	across layers. The cost — one extra writ per cartograph node and three
160	new writ types — is bounded; survey writs reach terminal phase quickly.
161	
162	### 3.3 Survey writs carry the rig's notes in `writ.body`
163	
164	| Pattern | `writ.body` is... | Set when |
165	|---|---|---|
166	| Mandate | the brief (instructions to the rig) | creation |
167	| Vision/charge/piece | the canonical content (prose, the thing itself) | creation/apply |
168	| Survey writ | the rig's notes (canonical content of the act of surveying) | rig completion |
169	
170	The survey writ IS the act of surveying. Its content IS the
171	surveyor's notes — the reasoning trace, the considered alternatives,
172	the rationale for the structural decisions made. Body is empty
173	pre-completion; the rig fills it on completion.
174	
175	### 3.4 Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only
176	
177	```typescript
178	interface SurveyDoc {
179	  id: string;             // primary key — the survey writ id
180	  targetNodeId: string;   // the cartograph node being surveyed
181	  rigName: string;        // 'survey-vision' | 'survey-charge' | 'survey-piece'
182	  rigVersion: string;     // for replay / diffing across rig versions
183	  surveyorId: string;     // which surveyor implementation ran
184	  completedAt: string;    // ISO timestamp
185	  // notes are NOT here — they live in writ.body
186	}
187	```
188	
189	Owned by the surveyor-apparatus substrate; shared across all surveyor
190	implementations.
191	
192	### 3.5 Immutability and supersedes
193	
194	Charges and pieces are rig-produced and never edited in place. When a
195	vision change cascades down such that an existing charge needs to
196	change, the rig creates a new charge with a `supersedes:
197	<oldChargeId>` link. The old charge stays in the books with its
198	content intact, marked superseded.
199	
200	| Substrate codifies | Surveyor implementation decides |
201	|---|---|
202	| `supersedes` link kind exists and is queryable | Whether to supersede vs keep |
203	| `cartograph.createCharge({ ..., supersedes })` API supports the link atomically | What threshold of vision-change triggers supersede |
204	| Re-survey rigs receive existing children as input context | How aggressive to be (rewrite vs conserve) |
205	| Superseded charges remain queryable; their dispatched work continues | Whether superseding cascades to children — substrate default: no auto-cascade |
206	| The survey writ records the rig's decision in its notes | Stop conditions, reasoning approach |
207	
208	Pieces follow the same mechanism. Mandates stay where they are.
209	
210	### 3.6 Substrate watches cartograph CDC — single-event-per-apply guarantee
211	
212	The substrate's observer subscribes to:
213	
214	- `book.cartograph.visions.created` / `.updated`
215	- `book.cartograph.charges.created` / `.updated`
216	- `book.cartograph.pieces.created`  / `.updated`
217	
218	On any event, it reads `ext['surveyor']` hints from the affected
219	node, creates the appropriate survey writ with `parentId` pointing
220	to the node, derives `ext['reckoner']` priority dimensions from the
221	hints + substrate defaults, and stamps to enter the petition queue.
222	
223	**Single-event-per-apply guarantee.** Apply CLI and surveyor rigs
224	MUST produce a single CDC event per logical operation, not separate
225	create+transition events. Concretely:
226	
227	- `cartograph.createVision` (and `createCharge`, `createPiece`) must
228	  accept the initial stage as a parameter and write writ + companion
229	  doc + final stage in one Stacks transaction, OR the apply CLI must
230	  wrap create + transition in one transaction at its layer.
231	- The substrate observer dedupes by writ id within a short window
232	  (defensive — should never matter if upstream is transactional, but
233	  catches integration bugs cheaply).
234	
235	Without this, a single `nsg vision apply` produces two CDC fires
236	(create + transition-to-active) and the substrate creates two
237	identical survey writs — wasted Reckoner cycles and duplicate rig
238	dispatches. This is a correctness requirement, not an optimization.
239	
240	### 3.7 Substrate + extension + default plugin shape
241	
242	Three plugins, mirroring the Reckoner pattern:
243	
244	1. **`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`** *(shipped)* — pure data layer.
245	   Vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs + ladder-invariant
246	   API. Does not own surveying.
247	
248	2. **`@shardworks/surveyor-apparatus`** *(substrate)*
249	   - Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`
250	   - Owns the surveyor registry (kit-contribution surface)
251	   - Owns the CDC observer
252	   - Owns the `ext['surveyor']` slot
253	   - Owns the rig-name convention (`survey-vision/charge/piece`)
254	   - Routes accepted survey petitions to the registered surveyor's
255	     rig templates
256	   - Stamps SurveyDoc on completion (rig fills `writ.body`; substrate
257	     wraps the writ)
258	   - Provides the surveyor anima tool surface (see §3.9)
259	   - Does NOT ship a concrete surveyor.
260	
261	3. **`@shardworks/scaffold-surveyor`** *(default)*
262	   - Registers a surveyor with the substrate via kit contribution
263	   - Provides minimal LLM-driven rig templates for each cartograph
264	     layer
265	   - Useful immediately; designed to be replaced as approaches are
266	     iterated
267	
268	### 3.8 Spider's dispatchable type set extends
269	
270	Spider's dispatchable type set grows to include survey writ types in
271	addition to mandates. Existing dispatch mechanics compose unchanged —
272	Spider already knows how to read writ type and resolve a rig
273	template; the extension is the registry the substrate provides for
274	discovering the registered surveyor's templates dynamically.
275	
276	### 3.9 Tool surface for surveyor anima — atomic create-with-hints
277	
278	Anima rigs need atomic operations that bundle the cartograph create
279	call, the `ext['surveyor']` stamping, and any `supersedes` link in
280	one tool call wrapped in a Stacks transaction. The surveyor-apparatus
281	contributes these tools.
282	
283	#### Tools (callableBy: `['surveyor']`)
284	
285	```
286	surveyor.create_charge / create_charges       # for survey-vision rigs
287	surveyor.create_piece  / create_pieces        # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
288	surveyor.create_mandate / create_mandates     # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
289	```
290	
291	Each rig template's tool catalogue includes only the layer-relevant
292	subset.
293	
294	**Single-create shape (charge example):**
295	
296	```typescript
297	interface CreateChargeArgs {
298	  parentId: string;              // vision id
299	  title: string;
300	  body: string;
301	  codex?: string;
302	  hints?: SurveyorExt;           // { severity, deadline, decay, complexity }
303	  supersedes?: string;           // optional — if replacing an existing charge
304	}
305	```
306	
307	Internally wraps a single `stacks.transaction`:
308	1. `cartograph.createCharge` (typed API)
309	2. `clerk.setWritExt('surveyor', hints)` if hints present
310	3. `clerk.linkWrits(newId, supersedes, 'supersedes')` if supersedes present
311	
312	**Batch-create shape:**
313	
314	```typescript
315	interface CreateChargesArgs {
316	  parentId: string;              // common parent — all in one batch share it
317	  charges: Array<{
318	    title: string;
319	    body: string;
320	    codex?: string;
321	    hints?: SurveyorExt;
322	    supersedes?: string;
323	  }>;
324	}
325	```
326	
327	Common parent enforced per batch: all children in one batch are
328	siblings under a single parent. Matches how surveys actually
329	decompose.
330	
331	**Mandate-create shape — direct ext.reckoner stamping:**
332	
333	```typescript
334	interface CreateMandateArgs {
335	  parentId: string;              // charge or piece
336	  title: string;
337	  body: string;                  // the brief content
338	  codex?: string;
339	  reckonerHints?: ReckonerExtRequest;  // priority dimensions for Reckoner gating
340	}
341	```
342	
343	Mandates aren't surveyed, so they skip `ext['surveyor']`. The tool
344	calls `reckoner.petition(writId, ext)` (stamp-only form) directly.
345	
346	#### Read tools
347	
348	The cartograph's existing `show`/`list` tools serve surveyor anima
349	needs; the only change is widening `callableBy` to include
350	`'surveyor'`.
351	
352	### 3.10 Priority signals — substrate defaults + patron flags + rig hints
353	
354	Priority dimensions for survey petitions are layered:
355	
356	```
357	substrate defaults  ◄──  rig hints (ext['surveyor'])  ◄──  patron CLI flags
358	       (vision-apply only; merges into ext['surveyor'])
359	```
360	
361	For visions, the patron sets priority via `nsg vision apply` CLI
362	flags (or sidecar fields). For charges and pieces, the surveyor rig
363	that creates the new node sets `ext['surveyor']` hints during
364	creation — the rig has the most context about this particular
365	charge/piece's importance. The substrate reads `ext['surveyor']` when
366	emitting each layer's survey petition.
367	
368	#### Patron CLI surface
369	
370	```
371	nsg vision apply <slug>
372	   [--severity moderate|serious|critical]    # default: moderate
373	   [--deadline <ISO-date>]                   # default: none
374	   [--decay]                                  # default: false
375	```
376	
377	#### Substrate dimension defaults (when hints absent)
378	
379	| Dimension | Default | Patron-overridable? | Rig-overridable? |
380	|---|---|---|---|
381	| `visionRelation` | `vision-advancer` for fresh; `vision-violator` for re-survey detecting contradiction | No | Yes (rig assesses on re-survey) |
382	| `severity` | `moderate` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].severity`) |
383	| `scope` | layer-based: vision=`major-area`, charge=`minor-area`, piece=`trivial` | No | No (substrate-derived from layer) |
384	| `time.decay` | `false` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].decay`) |
385	| `time.deadline` | `null` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].deadline`) |
386	| `domain` | `['planning']` | No | No |
387	| `complexity` | `bounded` | No | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].complexity`) |
388	
389	#### `ext['surveyor']` shape
390	
391	```typescript
392	interface SurveyorExt {
393	  severity?: 'moderate' | 'serious' | 'critical';
394	  deadline?: string;       // ISO date
395	  decay?: boolean;
396	  complexity?: 'bounded' | 'unbounded';
397	}
398	```
399	
400	Substrate-owned plugin-keyed slot on `writ.ext`. The vision-apply
401	CLI writes this for visions; surveyor rigs write this for charges
402	and pieces when they create the new cartograph node.
403	
404	---
405	
406	## 4. Worked example — cake-bakery vision through to mandates
407	
408	A concrete walk-through of the cascade.
409	
410	### Phase 1 — Patron creates the vision
411	
412	Patron creates the file tree:
413	
414	```
415	<GUILD>/vision/cake-bakery/
416	  vision.md
417	  vision-metadata.yml
418	```
419	
420	`vision.md`:
421	```markdown
422	# Cake Bakery
423	
424	A small online storefront for our bakery. Customers should be able to
425	browse our cakes, place orders for pickup or delivery, pay via Stripe,
426	and track their order status. Internal staff need to see the queue of
427	pending orders.
428	
429	The launch deadline is 2026-06-15 — we have a partner doing a press
430	release that day.
431	```
432	
433	`vision-metadata.yml`:
434	```yaml
435	stage: active
436	codex: bakery-app
437	```
438	
439	Patron runs:
440	```
441	nsg vision apply cake-bakery --severity serious --deadline 2026-06-15
442	```
443	
444	The CLI:
445	- Reads sidecar — no `visionId` → first apply
446	- Calls `cartograph.createVision({ title: 'Cake Bakery', body: <vision.md content>, codex: 'bakery-app', stage: 'active' })` — single transaction lands writ + doc + active stage
447	- Receives `vis-1`
448	- Writes `ext['surveyor']` on `vis-1`:
449	  ```json
450	  { "severity": "serious", "deadline": "2026-06-15", "decay": false }
451	  ```
452	- Writes `visionId: vis-1` back into the sidecar
453	
454	State after Phase 1:
455	- `vis-1` writ exists, `phase: open`, `stage: active`
456	- `vis-1.body` carries the vision prose
457	- `vis-1.ext.surveyor = { severity: 'serious', deadline: '2026-06-15' }`
458	- Sidecar has `visionId: vis-1`
459	
460	### Phase 2 — Surveyor-apparatus emits the initial petition
461	
462	CDC fires once (per the single-event-per-apply guarantee). The
463	surveyor-apparatus observer wakes:
464	
465	- Reads `vis-1`'s current state and `ext['surveyor']`
466	- Creates `surv-1` (a `survey-vision` writ):
467	  - `parentId: vis-1`
468	  - `body: ''` (rig will fill on completion)
469	  - `phase: new`
470	- Stamps `ext['reckoner']` derived from hints + defaults:
471	  ```json
472	  {
473	    "source": "scaffold-surveyor.survey-vision",
474	    "priority": {
475	      "visionRelation": "vision-advancer",
476	      "severity": "serious",
477	      "scope": "major-area",
478	      "time": { "decay": false, "deadline": "2026-06-15" },
479	      "domain": ["planning"]
480	    },
481	    "complexity": "bounded"
482	  }
483	  ```
484	
485	### Phase 3 — Reckoner accepts; Spider dispatches; rig surveys vision
486	
487	Next Reckoner tick:
488	- Picks up `surv-1`
489	- Default scheduler: always-approve
490	- Transitions `surv-1` to `phase: open`
491	
492	Spider's next pass:
493	- Sees `surv-1`: type `survey-vision`, phase `open`, no rig yet
494	- Resolves rig template via the surveyor registry → scaffold-surveyor's
495	  `survey-vision` template
496	- Dispatches; Animator launches an anima session
497	
498	The rig:
499	- Reads `vis-1.body` (the vision prose)
500	- Reads `vis-1`'s existing children (none — first survey)
501	- Reasons about decomposition. Cake-bakery falls along customer-journey
502	  lines: Browse + order, Payment, Fulfillment, Internal staff queue.
503	- Calls `surveyor.create_charges` with four charges (single batch, single
504	  transaction):
505	
506	  ```
507	  ch-1  Browse + order placement   hints: { severity: 'serious' }
508	  ch-2  Payment processing         hints: { severity: 'serious', deadline: '2026-06-15' }
509	  ch-3  Order status / fulfillment hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
510	  ch-4  Internal staff queue       hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
511	  ```
512	
513	- Writes notes into `surv-1.body`:
514	  ```markdown
515	  # Survey of cake-bakery vision (vis-1)
516	
517	  Decomposed along customer-journey + internal-tooling axes — chose
518	  this over technical layers (frontend/backend/db) because the vision
519	  emphasizes observable outcomes per actor, not architecture.
520	
521	  Charges created:
522	  - ch-1: Browse + order placement (severity: serious — foundational)
523	  - ch-2: Payment processing (severity: serious; deadline inherited
524	          from vision)
525	  - ch-3: Order status / fulfillment (severity: moderate — depends on
526	          ch-1 + ch-2 being live before fulfillment can be exercised)
527	  - ch-4: Internal staff queue view (severity: moderate)
528	
529	  Considered axes rejected: technical-layer decomposition (would split
530	  observable outcomes across charges); per-feature (too granular for
531	  charge layer — those become pieces).
532	  ```
533	
534	- Completes `surv-1`
535	
536	State after Phase 3:
537	- `surv-1` terminal, body holds reasoning trace
538	- `ch-1`–`ch-4` exist as cartograph charges
539	- Each has `ext['surveyor']` priority hints
540	
541	### Phase 4 — Cascade to charge layer
542	
543	CDC fires four times (one per new charge). Substrate observer creates
544	four survey-charge writs (`surv-2`–`surv-5`). Reckoner accepts in
545	priority order.
546	
547	For `ch-2` (Payment processing), the rig creates pieces:
548	```
549	pc-1  Stripe SDK integration       hints: { severity: 'serious' }
550	pc-2  Refund handling              hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
551	pc-3  Receipt + confirmation emails hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
552	```
553	
554	For `ch-1` (Browse + order placement), the rig might decide it's
555	already concrete enough and create mandates directly via
556	`surveyor.create_mandates`.
557	
558	### Phase 5 — Cascade to piece layer
559	
560	For each piece created in Phase 4, CDC fires → substrate creates
561	survey-piece writ → Reckoner gates → Spider dispatches surveyor rig.
562	
563	For `pc-1` (Stripe SDK integration), the rig creates mandates:
564	```
565	m-1  Install + configure stripe SDK
566	m-2  Create /api/checkout endpoint
567	m-3  Create /api/webhooks/stripe endpoint
568	m-4  Add Stripe key management to env config
569	```
570	
571	Each mandate has full brief content written by the rig (the rig is
572	assembling the brief at this layer; it has the context to do so).
573	Mandate priorities are stamped via direct `ext['reckoner']` (no
574	surveying-of-mandates).
575	
576	### Phase 6 — Mandates execute
577	
578	Each mandate runs through the existing implementer pipeline unchanged:
579	Reckoner accepts based on scheduler + capacity → Spider dispatches
580	implementer rig → rig writes code, commits, seals, pushes.
581	
582	### Phase 7 — Patron walkthrough at the charge layer
583	
584	As a charge's children all complete, the patron walks through it. The
585	charge is the patron-contract boundary — the unit of validation. The
586	walkthrough surface itself is a separate design problem (see Open
587	Questions §5.5).
588	
589	### Phase 8 — Vision completion
590	
591	When all charges are validated, the patron transitions the vision
592	to its terminal state. Auto-completion is not provided.
593	
594	### What got persisted across the whole flow
595	
596	- 1 vision writ, updated multiple times across stage transitions
597	- ~5 survey writs, each terminal with notes preserved
598	- 4 charges + ~7 pieces (estimate) + ~25 mandates (estimate)
599	- Each survey writ's `body` is a readable record of the rig's
600	  reasoning at that layer
601	- Each cartograph node's `ext['surveyor']` records its priority intent
602	- The full cascade is queryable via `parentId` walks
603	
604	---
605	
606	## 5. Open design questions
607	
608	The architecture above is settled. These remain open and are tracked
609	as design clicks in the sanctum.
610	
611	### 5.1 Stage gating on visions
612	
613	Should drafts trigger surveys? v0 lean: every apply triggers
614	surveying; draft-without-surveying layers on later if it becomes a
615	real workflow need.
616	
617	### 5.2 Dynamic rig template registration
618	
619	Spider currently resolves rigs via static `rigTemplateMappings` keyed
620	by writ type. Surveyor implementations need to register rig templates
621	at plugin load time. Strongest fit: dynamic kit-contribution
622	registration matching the Reckoner-scheduler pattern. Prerequisite:
623	audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism for discrimination
624	across writ "kinds" (implementer/spec/reviewer/surveyor).
625	
626	### 5.3 One survey writ type vs three
627	
628	Symmetric is decided; the sub-question is whether `survey-vision`,
629	`survey-charge`, `survey-piece` are three writ types or one
630	`survey { targetType: ... }` type. v0 lean: three (matches
631	cartograph's per-layer types; Spider's static-config dispatch is
632	direct).
633	
634	### 5.4 Default scaffold-surveyor design
635	
636	What does the default surveyor actually do? Open: model selection,
637	prompt structure, stop conditions (skip pieces and emit mandates
638	directly?), how the rig decides priority hints, re-survey behavior
639	(diff against prior surveys?).
640	
641	### 5.5 Mandate creation by surveyor rigs — priority discipline
642	
643	Surveyor rigs stamp `ext['reckoner']` directly when creating mandates.
644	There's no constraint mechanism — rigs could over-claim priority
645	(everything `serious`). Operational tuning rather than substrate
646	mechanism for v0; revisit if observed drift.
647	
648	### 5.6 Substrate scope detail
649	
650	Where exactly does the substrate / surveyor-implementation boundary
651	fall for ergonomic concerns (priority defaults, source-id grammar,
652	role-file convention, etc.)?
653	
654	### 5.7 Plural surveyors — future
655	
656	When multiple surveyor implementations are registered, how does the
657	substrate select? Per-vision config? Per-cartograph-tree?
658	Round-robin? Default-with-override? Not for v0 — only one surveyor
659	registered.
660	
661	### 5.8 Cascading supersedes — children of superseded nodes
662	
663	Substrate default: superseded charges' pieces stay attached to the
664	superseded parent. Patron ergonomics caveat: walkthrough surfaces
665	orphaned children for resolution. A "soft cancel" CLI surface
666	(`nsg charge cancel ch-3 --cascade`) may be earlier than v0+1.
667	
668	### 5.9 Vision completion criteria
669	
670	When does a vision complete? Some-validated-some-dropped, all
671	validated, patron-explicit? v0 lean: completion is purely a patron
672	action via `nsg vision transition`; no auto-completion.
673	
674	### 5.10 Survey rig failure handling
675	
676	Spider has retry semantics for mandates. Do they compose with survey
677	writ types? Lean: Spider's existing retry semantics apply uniformly
678	across dispatchable types. Confirm during the substrate commission
679	audit.
680	
681	### 5.11 Patron walkthrough CLI surface
682	
683	The patron-contract validation step at the charge layer is undesigned
684	in this document. Walkthrough is a substantial design problem — how
685	delivered work is presented, what the validation interaction looks
686	like, how feedback feeds back into the vision file. Surfaced as a
687	known gap, not blocking the cascade.
688	
689	### 5.12 Per-charge priority nudge
690	
691	Patron sets `--severity` at vision-apply time. No per-charge nudge
692	CLI. Future surface: `nsg charge nudge ch-2 --severity serious`.
693	
694	### 5.13 Sidecar file organization
695	
696	The sidecar mixes system-managed (`visionId`) and patron-managed
697	(`stage`, `codex`, priority hints) fields. v0 lean: keep merged;
698	document `visionId` as system-managed. Cleanup option later: split
699	into hidden system file + patron metadata file.
700	
701	### 5.14 Zero-children survey rig behavior
702	
703	A rig may complete with no children created ("too vague",
704	"sufficient as-is"). The substrate has no special handling — survey
705	writ terminates with notes only; cartograph node sits without further
706	decomposition. Lean: zero-children is a valid outcome; document and
707	optionally surface in the dashboard.
708	
709	### 5.15 Re-survey upstream-cascade behavior
710	
711	If the patron edits the vision in a way that changes the meaning of
712	existing charges *but doesn't change the charges' content* (the
713	upstream survey rig didn't supersede them), the children of those
714	charges don't get re-surveyed — the substrate's observer only fires
715	on changes to the *node being decomposed*, not upstream of it.
716	
717	This is structurally correct (immutability) but may surprise patrons.
718	Real remediation: the patron edits the vision in a way that forces
719	the upstream rig to supersede the charge.
720	
721	---
722	
723	## Related documents
724	
725	- [Reckoner Contract](petitioner-registration.md) — how
726	  `ext['reckoner']` works, how the Reckoner gates petitions, the
727	  scheduler kit-contribution surface.
728	- [Reckonings Book](reckonings-book.md) — Reckoner's evaluation log.
729	- [Cartograph plugin README](../../packages/plugins/cartograph/README.md)
730	  — vision/charge/piece writ types, companion docs, the
731	  CartographApi.
732	- [Guild Metaphor](../guild-metaphor.md) — vocabulary including the
733	  Surveyor's role.
734

file.content:
# The Surveying Cascade — Patron-Vision Authoring through Mandate Dispatch

Status: **Draft**

> **⚠️ Cross-apparatus design.** This document defines the
> end-to-end cascade by which patron-authored product visions become
> commissionable mandate work, spanning three apparatuses: the
> Cartograph (data layer, shipped), the Surveyor (substrate +
> extension + default, not yet built), and the Reckoner (petition
> scheduler, shipped). It is a settled architectural reference; the
> open questions section names the design problems that remain.

---

## Scope

The cascade covers:

- **Vision authoring on disk** — patrons author visions as `vision.md`
  files in a guild directory tree; `nsg vision apply` snapshots them
  into the cartograph.
- **Recursive surveying** — typed survey writs at every cartograph
  layer (vision/charge/piece) gate the work of decomposing one node
  into its children, using the Reckoner for scheduling.
- **Mandate emission** — surveying piece-layer (or charge-layer)
  rigs ultimately produce mandates, which dispatch through the
  existing implementer pipeline unchanged.

Out of scope: the patron walkthrough surface (the patron-contract
validation step at the charge layer) is acknowledged as a
substantial design problem but designed elsewhere.

---

## 1. Vocabulary

| Concept | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Vision** | Long-lived patron intent. Authored on disk; snapshotted into a writ + VisionDoc by the cartograph. |
| **Charge** | First decomposition under a vision. Patron-contract boundary; the unit of patron walkthrough. Does NOT self-nest. |
| **Piece** | Recursive internal organization under a charge. Self-nests. No patron contract. |
| **Mandate** | Existing leaf writ; dispatched by Spider via the existing implementer pipeline. |
| **Survey writ** (`survey-vision`/`survey-charge`/`survey-piece`) | Typed writ that captures one act of surveying one cartograph node. `writ.body` holds the rig's notes (set on rig completion). One survey writ per surveying event. |
| **SurveyDoc** | Companion doc for a survey writ, keyed by writ id. Holds envelope metadata only — the rig's notes live in `writ.body`. |
| **Surveyor** | The role / agent / apparatus that performs surveying. The substrate provides the cascade machinery; concrete surveyor implementations register their rig templates as kit contributions. |
| **`ext['surveyor']`** | Plugin-keyed `writ.ext` slot owned by the surveyor-apparatus. Carries priority hints (severity, deadline, decay, complexity) on cartograph nodes — set by the patron via apply CLI for visions, set by the upstream surveyor rig for charges and pieces. The substrate translates these into Reckoner dimensions when emitting the survey petition. |

> **Naming note.** "The Surveyor" was previously reserved in the
> framework architecture for a codex-awareness apparatus. That use is
> superseded by the cartograph-decomposition meaning defined here. The
> alias registry in the sanctum carries the substitution.

---

## 2. End-to-end flow

```
PATRON
  │ writes vision.md + sidecar in <GUILD>/vision/<slug>/
  │
  │ runs `nsg vision apply <slug> [--severity ...] [--deadline ...]`
  ▼
CARTOGRAPH (data layer)
  │ creates or updates the vision writ + VisionDoc (one transaction)
  │ writes ext['surveyor'] priority hints from CLI flags + sidecar
  │ emits Stacks CDC: book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}
  ▼
SURVEYOR-APPARATUS (substrate)
  │ CDC observer fires
  │ reads ext['surveyor'] hints from the cartograph node
  │ creates a survey-vision writ in `new` phase, parentId = the vision
  │ stamps ext['reckoner'] (priority dimensions derived from hints + defaults)
  ▼
RECKONER
  │ holds petition, evaluates per tick
  │ dependency-aware gating; scheduler decides accept/decline/defer
  │ on accept: clerk.transition → survey-vision writ moves to `open`
  ▼
SPIDER (extended to dispatch survey-* writ types)
  │ resolves rig template via the surveyor registry
  │ dispatches to the registered surveyor's rig
  ▼
SURVEYOR RIG (provided by the registered surveyor implementation)
  │ reads parent (vision) via writ.parentId
  │ reads existing children if any (re-survey case)
  │ reasons about decomposition
  │ creates child charges via cartograph API + sets ext['surveyor'] hints
  │ writes its notes into survey-vision writ.body
  │ completes the survey-vision writ
  ▼
[CASCADE]
  │ each new charge fires CDC → substrate creates survey-charge writ
  │ → recursion descends one layer
  ▼
LEAF (mandate)
  │ pieces eventually elaborate to mandates
  │ mandates dispatch through the existing implementer pipeline
```

---

## 3. Architecture

### 3.1 Vision authoring on disk

The patron's authoring surface is a directory tree:

```
<GUILD>/vision/
  <slug>/
    vision.md             ← prose, patron-edited
    vision-metadata.yml   ← sidecar; carries `visionId` after first apply
```

- The slug is a directory affordance, not a durable identity. After
  first apply, the sidecar's `visionId` field binds the file tree to
  its cartograph writ — written by the system, never edited by hand.
- `nsg vision apply <slug>` is a single code path for both first
  import and Nth re-import: locate sidecar, resolve bound writ via
  `visionId` (or create new), copy prose into `writ.body`, sync
  stage/codex via `transitionVision`, write priority hints into
  `ext['surveyor']`, and on first run write the new id back into the
  sidecar.
- Data flow is **one-way: file → writ**. The writ is a snapshot of
  patron intent; the file is the editable source.
- Stale-binding (file deleted, writ remains): warn at scan, error on
  next apply attempt; the patron transitions the writ to
  sunset/cancelled explicitly.

### 3.2 Symmetric typed-writ-driven cascade

Surveying happens through dedicated survey writs at every cartograph
layer. The cascade has a uniform shape:

1. A cartograph node lands (vision via apply; charge or piece via an
   upstream survey rig).
2. CDC fires on the cartograph book — `created` or `updated`.
3. The surveyor-apparatus's observer reads `ext['surveyor']` from the
   node and creates the appropriate survey writ in `new` phase with
   `parentId` set to the cartograph node. Priority hints translate
   into Reckoner dimensions.
4. The observer stamps `ext['reckoner']` to enter the petition queue.
5. Reckoner gates per its existing rules.
6. On accept, Spider dispatches the registered surveyor's rig.
7. Rig reads the parent (and existing children, on re-survey),
   reasons about decomposition, creates children via the surveyor
   tool surface (which atomically creates the cartograph node and
   stamps its `ext['surveyor']`), writes its notes into the survey
   writ's `body`, completes.
8. Each child cartograph node fires fresh CDC → loop to step 2 one
   layer down.

Why symmetric (separate survey writs at every layer) over asymmetric
(rigs bound directly to charge/piece writs): notes have a clean home
(`writ.body` of a dedicated writ per surveying event); charge/piece
writ lifecycle stays clean (their phase is about contract lifecycle,
not about whether decomposition has happened); future re-survey of an
existing charge is structurally trivial; substrate code is uniform
across layers. The cost — one extra writ per cartograph node and three
new writ types — is bounded; survey writs reach terminal phase quickly.

### 3.3 Survey writs carry the rig's notes in `writ.body`

| Pattern | `writ.body` is... | Set when |
|---|---|---|
| Mandate | the brief (instructions to the rig) | creation |
| Vision/charge/piece | the canonical content (prose, the thing itself) | creation/apply |
| Survey writ | the rig's notes (canonical content of the act of surveying) | rig completion |

The survey writ IS the act of surveying. Its content IS the
surveyor's notes — the reasoning trace, the considered alternatives,
the rationale for the structural decisions made. Body is empty
pre-completion; the rig fills it on completion.

### 3.4 Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only

```typescript
interface SurveyDoc {
  id: string;             // primary key — the survey writ id
  targetNodeId: string;   // the cartograph node being surveyed
  rigName: string;        // 'survey-vision' | 'survey-charge' | 'survey-piece'
  rigVersion: string;     // for replay / diffing across rig versions
  surveyorId: string;     // which surveyor implementation ran
  completedAt: string;    // ISO timestamp
  // notes are NOT here — they live in writ.body
}
```

Owned by the surveyor-apparatus substrate; shared across all surveyor
implementations.

### 3.5 Immutability and supersedes

Charges and pieces are rig-produced and never edited in place. When a
vision change cascades down such that an existing charge needs to
change, the rig creates a new charge with a `supersedes:
<oldChargeId>` link. The old charge stays in the books with its
content intact, marked superseded.

| Substrate codifies | Surveyor implementation decides |
|---|---|
| `supersedes` link kind exists and is queryable | Whether to supersede vs keep |
| `cartograph.createCharge({ ..., supersedes })` API supports the link atomically | What threshold of vision-change triggers supersede |
| Re-survey rigs receive existing children as input context | How aggressive to be (rewrite vs conserve) |
| Superseded charges remain queryable; their dispatched work continues | Whether superseding cascades to children — substrate default: no auto-cascade |
| The survey writ records the rig's decision in its notes | Stop conditions, reasoning approach |

Pieces follow the same mechanism. Mandates stay where they are.

### 3.6 Substrate watches cartograph CDC — single-event-per-apply guarantee

The substrate's observer subscribes to:

- `book.cartograph.visions.created` / `.updated`
- `book.cartograph.charges.created` / `.updated`
- `book.cartograph.pieces.created`  / `.updated`

On any event, it reads `ext['surveyor']` hints from the affected
node, creates the appropriate survey writ with `parentId` pointing
to the node, derives `ext['reckoner']` priority dimensions from the
hints + substrate defaults, and stamps to enter the petition queue.

**Single-event-per-apply guarantee.** Apply CLI and surveyor rigs
MUST produce a single CDC event per logical operation, not separate
create+transition events. Concretely:

- `cartograph.createVision` (and `createCharge`, `createPiece`) must
  accept the initial stage as a parameter and write writ + companion
  doc + final stage in one Stacks transaction, OR the apply CLI must
  wrap create + transition in one transaction at its layer.
- The substrate observer dedupes by writ id within a short window
  (defensive — should never matter if upstream is transactional, but
  catches integration bugs cheaply).

Without this, a single `nsg vision apply` produces two CDC fires
(create + transition-to-active) and the substrate creates two
identical survey writs — wasted Reckoner cycles and duplicate rig
dispatches. This is a correctness requirement, not an optimization.

### 3.7 Substrate + extension + default plugin shape

Three plugins, mirroring the Reckoner pattern:

1. **`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`** *(shipped)* — pure data layer.
   Vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs + ladder-invariant
   API. Does not own surveying.

2. **`@shardworks/surveyor-apparatus`** *(substrate)*
   - Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`
   - Owns the surveyor registry (kit-contribution surface)
   - Owns the CDC observer
   - Owns the `ext['surveyor']` slot
   - Owns the rig-name convention (`survey-vision/charge/piece`)
   - Routes accepted survey petitions to the registered surveyor's
     rig templates
   - Stamps SurveyDoc on completion (rig fills `writ.body`; substrate
     wraps the writ)
   - Provides the surveyor anima tool surface (see §3.9)
   - Does NOT ship a concrete surveyor.

3. **`@shardworks/scaffold-surveyor`** *(default)*
   - Registers a surveyor with the substrate via kit contribution
   - Provides minimal LLM-driven rig templates for each cartograph
     layer
   - Useful immediately; designed to be replaced as approaches are
     iterated

### 3.8 Spider's dispatchable type set extends

Spider's dispatchable type set grows to include survey writ types in
addition to mandates. Existing dispatch mechanics compose unchanged —
Spider already knows how to read writ type and resolve a rig
template; the extension is the registry the substrate provides for
discovering the registered surveyor's templates dynamically.

### 3.9 Tool surface for surveyor anima — atomic create-with-hints

Anima rigs need atomic operations that bundle the cartograph create
call, the `ext['surveyor']` stamping, and any `supersedes` link in
one tool call wrapped in a Stacks transaction. The surveyor-apparatus
contributes these tools.

#### Tools (callableBy: `['surveyor']`)

```
surveyor.create_charge / create_charges       # for survey-vision rigs
surveyor.create_piece  / create_pieces        # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
surveyor.create_mandate / create_mandates     # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
```

Each rig template's tool catalogue includes only the layer-relevant
subset.

**Single-create shape (charge example):**

```typescript
interface CreateChargeArgs {
  parentId: string;              // vision id
  title: string;
  body: string;
  codex?: string;
  hints?: SurveyorExt;           // { severity, deadline, decay, complexity }
  supersedes?: string;           // optional — if replacing an existing charge
}
```

Internally wraps a single `stacks.transaction`:
1. `cartograph.createCharge` (typed API)
2. `clerk.setWritExt('surveyor', hints)` if hints present
3. `clerk.linkWrits(newId, supersedes, 'supersedes')` if supersedes present

**Batch-create shape:**

```typescript
interface CreateChargesArgs {
  parentId: string;              // common parent — all in one batch share it
  charges: Array<{
    title: string;
    body: string;
    codex?: string;
    hints?: SurveyorExt;
    supersedes?: string;
  }>;
}
```

Common parent enforced per batch: all children in one batch are
siblings under a single parent. Matches how surveys actually
decompose.

**Mandate-create shape — direct ext.reckoner stamping:**

```typescript
interface CreateMandateArgs {
  parentId: string;              // charge or piece
  title: string;
  body: string;                  // the brief content
  codex?: string;
  reckonerHints?: ReckonerExtRequest;  // priority dimensions for Reckoner gating
}
```

Mandates aren't surveyed, so they skip `ext['surveyor']`. The tool
calls `reckoner.petition(writId, ext)` (stamp-only form) directly.

#### Read tools

The cartograph's existing `show`/`list` tools serve surveyor anima
needs; the only change is widening `callableBy` to include
`'surveyor'`.

### 3.10 Priority signals — substrate defaults + patron flags + rig hints

Priority dimensions for survey petitions are layered:

```
substrate defaults  ◄──  rig hints (ext['surveyor'])  ◄──  patron CLI flags
       (vision-apply only; merges into ext['surveyor'])
```

For visions, the patron sets priority via `nsg vision apply` CLI
flags (or sidecar fields). For charges and pieces, the surveyor rig
that creates the new node sets `ext['surveyor']` hints during
creation — the rig has the most context about this particular
charge/piece's importance. The substrate reads `ext['surveyor']` when
emitting each layer's survey petition.

#### Patron CLI surface

```
nsg vision apply <slug>
   [--severity moderate|serious|critical]    # default: moderate
   [--deadline <ISO-date>]                   # default: none
   [--decay]                                  # default: false
```

#### Substrate dimension defaults (when hints absent)

| Dimension | Default | Patron-overridable? | Rig-overridable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `visionRelation` | `vision-advancer` for fresh; `vision-violator` for re-survey detecting contradiction | No | Yes (rig assesses on re-survey) |
| `severity` | `moderate` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].severity`) |
| `scope` | layer-based: vision=`major-area`, charge=`minor-area`, piece=`trivial` | No | No (substrate-derived from layer) |
| `time.decay` | `false` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].decay`) |
| `time.deadline` | `null` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].deadline`) |
| `domain` | `['planning']` | No | No |
| `complexity` | `bounded` | No | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].complexity`) |

#### `ext['surveyor']` shape

```typescript
interface SurveyorExt {
  severity?: 'moderate' | 'serious' | 'critical';
  deadline?: string;       // ISO date
  decay?: boolean;
  complexity?: 'bounded' | 'unbounded';
}
```

Substrate-owned plugin-keyed slot on `writ.ext`. The vision-apply
CLI writes this for visions; surveyor rigs write this for charges
and pieces when they create the new cartograph node.

---

## 4. Worked example — cake-bakery vision through to mandates

A concrete walk-through of the cascade.

### Phase 1 — Patron creates the vision

Patron creates the file tree:

```
<GUILD>/vision/cake-bakery/
  vision.md
  vision-metadata.yml
```

`vision.md`:
```markdown
# Cake Bakery

A small online storefront for our bakery. Customers should be able to
browse our cakes, place orders for pickup or delivery, pay via Stripe,
and track their order status. Internal staff need to see the queue of
pending orders.

The launch deadline is 2026-06-15 — we have a partner doing a press
release that day.
```

`vision-metadata.yml`:
```yaml
stage: active
codex: bakery-app
```

Patron runs:
```
nsg vision apply cake-bakery --severity serious --deadline 2026-06-15
```

The CLI:
- Reads sidecar — no `visionId` → first apply
- Calls `cartograph.createVision({ title: 'Cake Bakery', body: <vision.md content>, codex: 'bakery-app', stage: 'active' })` — single transaction lands writ + doc + active stage
- Receives `vis-1`
- Writes `ext['surveyor']` on `vis-1`:
  ```json
  { "severity": "serious", "deadline": "2026-06-15", "decay": false }
  ```
- Writes `visionId: vis-1` back into the sidecar

State after Phase 1:
- `vis-1` writ exists, `phase: open`, `stage: active`
- `vis-1.body` carries the vision prose
- `vis-1.ext.surveyor = { severity: 'serious', deadline: '2026-06-15' }`
- Sidecar has `visionId: vis-1`

### Phase 2 — Surveyor-apparatus emits the initial petition

CDC fires once (per the single-event-per-apply guarantee). The
surveyor-apparatus observer wakes:

- Reads `vis-1`'s current state and `ext['surveyor']`
- Creates `surv-1` (a `survey-vision` writ):
  - `parentId: vis-1`
  - `body: ''` (rig will fill on completion)
  - `phase: new`
- Stamps `ext['reckoner']` derived from hints + defaults:
  ```json
  {
    "source": "scaffold-surveyor.survey-vision",
    "priority": {
      "visionRelation": "vision-advancer",
      "severity": "serious",
      "scope": "major-area",
      "time": { "decay": false, "deadline": "2026-06-15" },
      "domain": ["planning"]
    },
    "complexity": "bounded"
  }
  ```

### Phase 3 — Reckoner accepts; Spider dispatches; rig surveys vision

Next Reckoner tick:
- Picks up `surv-1`
- Default scheduler: always-approve
- Transitions `surv-1` to `phase: open`

Spider's next pass:
- Sees `surv-1`: type `survey-vision`, phase `open`, no rig yet
- Resolves rig template via the surveyor registry → scaffold-surveyor's
  `survey-vision` template
- Dispatches; Animator launches an anima session

The rig:
- Reads `vis-1.body` (the vision prose)
- Reads `vis-1`'s existing children (none — first survey)
- Reasons about decomposition. Cake-bakery falls along customer-journey
  lines: Browse + order, Payment, Fulfillment, Internal staff queue.
- Calls `surveyor.create_charges` with four charges (single batch, single
  transaction):

  ```
  ch-1  Browse + order placement   hints: { severity: 'serious' }
  ch-2  Payment processing         hints: { severity: 'serious', deadline: '2026-06-15' }
  ch-3  Order status / fulfillment hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
  ch-4  Internal staff queue       hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
  ```

- Writes notes into `surv-1.body`:
  ```markdown
  # Survey of cake-bakery vision (vis-1)

  Decomposed along customer-journey + internal-tooling axes — chose
  this over technical layers (frontend/backend/db) because the vision
  emphasizes observable outcomes per actor, not architecture.

  Charges created:
  - ch-1: Browse + order placement (severity: serious — foundational)
  - ch-2: Payment processing (severity: serious; deadline inherited
          from vision)
  - ch-3: Order status / fulfillment (severity: moderate — depends on
          ch-1 + ch-2 being live before fulfillment can be exercised)
  - ch-4: Internal staff queue view (severity: moderate)

  Considered axes rejected: technical-layer decomposition (would split
  observable outcomes across charges); per-feature (too granular for
  charge layer — those become pieces).
  ```

- Completes `surv-1`

State after Phase 3:
- `surv-1` terminal, body holds reasoning trace
- `ch-1`–`ch-4` exist as cartograph charges
- Each has `ext['surveyor']` priority hints

### Phase 4 — Cascade to charge layer

CDC fires four times (one per new charge). Substrate observer creates
four survey-charge writs (`surv-2`–`surv-5`). Reckoner accepts in
priority order.

For `ch-2` (Payment processing), the rig creates pieces:
```
pc-1  Stripe SDK integration       hints: { severity: 'serious' }
pc-2  Refund handling              hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
pc-3  Receipt + confirmation emails hints: { severity: 'moderate' }
```

For `ch-1` (Browse + order placement), the rig might decide it's
already concrete enough and create mandates directly via
`surveyor.create_mandates`.

### Phase 5 — Cascade to piece layer

For each piece created in Phase 4, CDC fires → substrate creates
survey-piece writ → Reckoner gates → Spider dispatches surveyor rig.

For `pc-1` (Stripe SDK integration), the rig creates mandates:
```
m-1  Install + configure stripe SDK
m-2  Create /api/checkout endpoint
m-3  Create /api/webhooks/stripe endpoint
m-4  Add Stripe key management to env config
```

Each mandate has full brief content written by the rig (the rig is
assembling the brief at this layer; it has the context to do so).
Mandate priorities are stamped via direct `ext['reckoner']` (no
surveying-of-mandates).

### Phase 6 — Mandates execute

Each mandate runs through the existing implementer pipeline unchanged:
Reckoner accepts based on scheduler + capacity → Spider dispatches
implementer rig → rig writes code, commits, seals, pushes.

### Phase 7 — Patron walkthrough at the charge layer

As a charge's children all complete, the patron walks through it. The
charge is the patron-contract boundary — the unit of validation. The
walkthrough surface itself is a separate design problem (see Open
Questions §5.5).

### Phase 8 — Vision completion

When all charges are validated, the patron transitions the vision
to its terminal state. Auto-completion is not provided.

### What got persisted across the whole flow

- 1 vision writ, updated multiple times across stage transitions
- ~5 survey writs, each terminal with notes preserved
- 4 charges + ~7 pieces (estimate) + ~25 mandates (estimate)
- Each survey writ's `body` is a readable record of the rig's
  reasoning at that layer
- Each cartograph node's `ext['surveyor']` records its priority intent
- The full cascade is queryable via `parentId` walks

---

## 5. Open design questions

The architecture above is settled. These remain open and are tracked
as design clicks in the sanctum.

### 5.1 Stage gating on visions

Should drafts trigger surveys? v0 lean: every apply triggers
surveying; draft-without-surveying layers on later if it becomes a
real workflow need.

### 5.2 Dynamic rig template registration

Spider currently resolves rigs via static `rigTemplateMappings` keyed
by writ type. Surveyor implementations need to register rig templates
at plugin load time. Strongest fit: dynamic kit-contribution
registration matching the Reckoner-scheduler pattern. Prerequisite:
audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism for discrimination
across writ "kinds" (implementer/spec/reviewer/surveyor).

### 5.3 One survey writ type vs three

Symmetric is decided; the sub-question is whether `survey-vision`,
`survey-charge`, `survey-piece` are three writ types or one
`survey { targetType: ... }` type. v0 lean: three (matches
cartograph's per-layer types; Spider's static-config dispatch is
direct).

### 5.4 Default scaffold-surveyor design

What does the default surveyor actually do? Open: model selection,
prompt structure, stop conditions (skip pieces and emit mandates
directly?), how the rig decides priority hints, re-survey behavior
(diff against prior surveys?).

### 5.5 Mandate creation by surveyor rigs — priority discipline

Surveyor rigs stamp `ext['reckoner']` directly when creating mandates.
There's no constraint mechanism — rigs could over-claim priority
(everything `serious`). Operational tuning rather than substrate
mechanism for v0; revisit if observed drift.

### 5.6 Substrate scope detail

Where exactly does the substrate / surveyor-implementation boundary
fall for ergonomic concerns (priority defaults, source-id grammar,
role-file convention, etc.)?

### 5.7 Plural surveyors — future

When multiple surveyor implementations are registered, how does the
substrate select? Per-vision config? Per-cartograph-tree?
Round-robin? Default-with-override? Not for v0 — only one surveyor
registered.

### 5.8 Cascading supersedes — children of superseded nodes

Substrate default: superseded charges' pieces stay attached to the
superseded parent. Patron ergonomics caveat: walkthrough surfaces
orphaned children for resolution. A "soft cancel" CLI surface
(`nsg charge cancel ch-3 --cascade`) may be earlier than v0+1.

### 5.9 Vision completion criteria

When does a vision complete? Some-validated-some-dropped, all
validated, patron-explicit? v0 lean: completion is purely a patron
action via `nsg vision transition`; no auto-completion.

### 5.10 Survey rig failure handling

Spider has retry semantics for mandates. Do they compose with survey
writ types? Lean: Spider's existing retry semantics apply uniformly
across dispatchable types. Confirm during the substrate commission
audit.

### 5.11 Patron walkthrough CLI surface

The patron-contract validation step at the charge layer is undesigned
in this document. Walkthrough is a substantial design problem — how
delivered work is presented, what the validation interaction looks
like, how feedback feeds back into the vision file. Surfaced as a
known gap, not blocking the cascade.

### 5.12 Per-charge priority nudge

Patron sets `--severity` at vision-apply time. No per-charge nudge
CLI. Future surface: `nsg charge nudge ch-2 --severity serious`.

### 5.13 Sidecar file organization

The sidecar mixes system-managed (`visionId`) and patron-managed
(`stage`, `codex`, priority hints) fields. v0 lean: keep merged;
document `visionId` as system-managed. Cleanup option later: split
into hidden system file + patron metadata file.

### 5.14 Zero-children survey rig behavior

A rig may complete with no children created ("too vague",
"sufficient as-is"). The substrate has no special handling — survey
writ terminates with notes only; cartograph node sits without further
decomposition. Lean: zero-children is a valid outcome; document and
optionally surface in the dashboard.

### 5.15 Re-survey upstream-cascade behavior

If the patron edits the vision in a way that changes the meaning of
existing charges *but doesn't change the charges' content* (the
upstream survey rig didn't supersede them), the children of those
charges don't get re-surveyed — the substrate's observer only fires
on changes to the *node being decomposed*, not upstream of it.

This is structurally correct (immutability) but may surprise patrons.
Real remediation: the patron edits the vision in a way that forces
the upstream rig to supersede the charge.

---

## Related documents

- [Reckoner Contract](petitioner-registration.md) — how
  `ext['reckoner']` works, how the Reckoner gates petitions, the
  scheduler kit-contribution surface.
- [Reckonings Book](reckonings-book.md) — Reckoner's evaluation log.
- [Cartograph plugin README](../../packages/plugins/cartograph/README.md)
  — vision/charge/piece writ types, companion docs, the
  CartographApi.
- [Guild Metaphor](../guild-metaphor.md) — vocabulary including the
  Surveyor's role.
